project_manifest.operational_status: Weekday Theotokion tone-correction extended to all resolved Menaion commemorations rank 1–4 (v2.5). Great Lent weekday seasonal override layer implemented (v2.6): stichera, aposticha, paremiae, troparion, and Theotokion slots now render explicit Lenten rubrics on Great Lent weekdays (Clean Monday through Great Friday) when no Menaion troparion is resolved; Menaion-resolved commemorations during Lent retain their proper troparion and Theotokion. Holy Week structural override live (v2.7): Palm Sunday through Great Saturday emit explicit day-specific rubrics across the five target Vespers slots. Unified Horologion navigation live (v3.0): single horologion mode, generic sidebar office selector for Vespers and Small Compline, old split mode strings eliminated. Small Compline (Mikron Apodeipnon) baseline live (v3.0): fixed slots resolved from compline-fixed.json, troparion-of-the-day via existing Vespers troparion infrastructure. Triodion corpus deferred. First Hour (Prima) baseline live (v3.1): fixed corpus (usual-beginning, psalm-5, psalm-89, psalm-100, trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-first-hour) resolved from first-hour-fixed.json; troparion-of-the-day and first-hour-theotokion resolved dynamically; Theotokion remains a rubric stub (deferred). Third Hour (Tertia) baseline live (v3.2): fixed corpus (usual-beginning, psalm-16, psalm-24, psalm-50, trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-third-hour) resolved from third-hour-fixed.json; troparion-of-the-day and third-hour-theotokion resolved dynamically; Theotokion remains a rubric stub (deferred). Sixth Hour (Sexta) baseline live (v3.3): fixed corpus (usual-beginning, psalm-53, psalm-54, psalm-90, trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-sixth-hour) resolved from sixth-hour-fixed.json; troparion-of-the-day and sixth-hour-theotokion resolved dynamically; Theotokion remains a rubric stub (deferred). Ninth Hour (Nona) baseline live (v3.4): fixed corpus (usual-beginning, psalm-83, psalm-84, psalm-85, trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-ninth-hour) resolved from ninth-hour-fixed.json; troparion-of-the-day and ninth-hour-theotokion resolved dynamically; Theotokion remains a rubric stub (deferred). Horologion engine v3.5–v4.4 now live: feast-rank arbitration introduced (_resolveFeastOverrideContext(), v3.5); Little Hours feast troparion override (_resolveLittleHourTroparionSlot(), v3.6); Vespers stichera/aposticha festal override (_resolveFestalRubricOverrideSlot(), v3.7); Great Lent seasonal troparion layer for all four Little Hours (_resolveLittleHourSeasonalTroparionSlot(), v3.8); Great Lent seasonal troparion layer for Small Compline (_resolveComplineSeasonalTroparionSlot(), v3.9); office-specific ordinary fallback normalization (_normalizeOrdinaryTroparionFallbackForOffice(), v4.0); Little Hours Theotokion normalization and festal rubric arbitration (v4.1/v4.3); Small Compline festal Theotokion rubric arbitration (v4.4). Season-aware feast qualification rule (v3.6/v3.8 — VERIFIED): outside Great Lent weekdays, Menaion rank 1–4 qualifies as a feast override; during Great Lent weekdays (Clean Monday through Great Friday), only ranks 1–2 qualify. Consequence: ordinary rank 4 saint commemorations no longer displace the Lenten weekday seasonal path on Great Lent weekdays; major feasts (e.g., Annunciation rank 1) still override during Lent; rank 4 saint overrides function normally outside Lent. Menaion corpus integration complete (all 12 months, 369/370 resolved; 05-18 held intentionally as text-unavailable). Resolver integration verified at runtime: date resolution, month loading, rank selection, troparion extraction, text-unavailable handling, and feast override integration all confirmed correct. Pascha boundary and Bright Week tone suppression verified; Thomas Sunday tone resumption verified. Small Compline festal Theotokion arbitration verified. Tone computation year-rollback bug (_computeBaselineTone) corrected and verified. Horologion v5.0 — Triodion Great Lent weekday overlay live: _loadTriodionData() / _resolveTriodionTroparion() insert Triodion-backed text on Great Lent weekdays after feast arbitration. Corpus covers Weeks 1–6 (data/triodion/triodion-lenten-weekday.json); verified across multiple Lent weeks including feast-override precedence (Annunciation rank 1 still wins). Horologion v5.1 — Holy Week text overlay live: _loadHolyWeekData() / _resolveHolyWeekText() provide actual troparion text for all seven Holy Week days (data/triodion/holy-week/holy-week.json); stichera/aposticha/paremiae/theotokion remain as informed rubrics. Holy Week structurally separate from the Great Lent weekday layer. Verified: Holy Monday, Holy Wednesday, Holy Friday, Holy Saturday. Horologion v5.2 — Pentecostarion / Bright Week overlay live: _loadPentecostarionData() / _resolvePentecostarionText() provide Paschal stichera and aposticha text for Bright Week (data/pentecostarion/pentecostarion-bright-week.json). Troparion already resolved from vespers-troparion.json; kathisma and theotokion correctly omitted. Thomas Sunday returns to ordinary Sunday resurrectional path. Verified: Pascha, Bright Wednesday, Thomas Saturday, Thomas Sunday. Horologion v5.3 — Menaion text-unavailable audit: corpus is effectively complete at 369/370 resolved across all 12 months. The prior 'approximately 200 text-unavailable' count reflects an earlier state; the resolution pass was completed before this tranche. One intentional holdout remains: 05-18 (Venerable Peter of Caesarea in Cappadocia) — no confirmed Hapgood/OCA/Jordanville source. Verified: May 25, May 21 resolve as menaion-feast-troparion; May 18 degrades honestly as menaion-text-unavailable in both Vespers and Small Compline. Horologion v5.4 — diagnostics and UI polish: _horDiagnosticsEnabled flag and toggleHorologionDiagnostics() added to office-ui.js. _renderHorologionDiagnostics(item, escapeHtml) module-level helper wired into rubric, stichera, and fallback-text branches of _renderHorologionItem(). Diagnostics toggle button added to #generic-settings sidebar in index.html. When enabled, surfaces resolvedAs, type, tone, inferred layer for variable Horologion slots including: menaion-feast-troparion, menaion-text-unavailable, triodion-lenten-troparion, holy-week-troparion, bright-week-paschal-stichera, bright-week-paschal-aposticha, weekday-theme-rubric, paschal-troparion. Off by default; non-destructive to normal rendering. Horologion v5.5 — Vespers kathisma full-text integration: ordinary weekday Vespers kathismata now support full-text rendering where corpus exists; supported kathismata: 1, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Great Lent guard added — ordinary-cycle full kathisma text suppressed during Great Lent; returns great-lent-ordinary-cycle-inapplicable. Verified outcomes: ordinary weekday Vespers → type:'kathisma', resolvedAs:'ordinary-weekday-full-text'; Bright Week → bright-week-no-kathisma; Sunday Small Vespers → sunday-small-vespers-kathisma-ordinary-omitted. Horologion v5.6 — Orthros / Matins structural baseline: Orthros added as a real selectable office. Fixed baseline content added: Usual Beginning, Six Psalms, Praises Psalms, Great Doxology. god-is-the-lord / Alleluia path resolves across: ordinary, Great Lent weekday, Holy Week, Bright Week. Troparion path delegates correctly to existing seasonal / feast stack. Verified: ordinary weekday Orthros → orthros-god-is-the-lord-ordinary; Great Lent weekday → orthros-great-lent-alleluia-path; Holy Week → orthros-holy-week-alleluia-path; Bright Week → orthros-bright-week-paschal-path. Horologion v5.7 — Orthros kathisma and praises expansion: kathisma appointments upgraded from placeholders to explicit rubrics; Praises stichera upgraded from placeholder to differentiated rubric logic. Verified: 2026-05-04 Orthros — kathisma-first → orthros-ordinary-weekday-kathisma-appointment; kathisma-second → orthros-ordinary-weekday-kathisma-appointment; praises-stichera → orthros-ordinary-weekday-praises-stichera-rubric. Orthros improved from 15 implemented / 5 placeholders to 18 implemented / 2 placeholders. Horologion v5.8 — Orthros sessional hymn / canon discipline: sessional-hymns and canon upgraded from vague placeholders to season-aware rubric logic. Verified cases now distinguish: ordinary weekday, Sunday, Great Lent weekday, Holy Week, Bright Week. Feast inheritance intact. Representative results: Holy Monday / Holy Wednesday → orthros-holy-week-sessional-hymns-rubric + orthros-holy-week-canon-rubric; Bright Monday / Bright Tuesday → orthros-bright-week-sessional-hymns-rubric + orthros-bright-week-canon-rubric; Tuesday after Thomas Sunday → orthros-ordinary-weekday-sessional-hymns-rubric + orthros-ordinary-weekday-canon-rubric. Orthros now resolves with 20 implemented / 0 placeholder slots.

open_bugs: 1. Rubric logic: [rite] placeholder resolution in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns. (LOW — see known_outstanding_issues.rite_placeholder_resolution)
architectural_debt: 1. Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js needs transition to a template system.

2. [CLOSED — v2.8.6] Hydration-race gating: renderOffice() is guarded by isHydrationComplete. selectMode() awaits hydration before setting that flag and calling requestRender(). requestRender()/flushRender() coalesce and serialise renders via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration. Residual: no automated regression test covers this gating path.
3. Redundant liturgical fragments (Gloria Patri/Kyrie) should be normalized into common.json.

4. Saints data model: identities.json + commemorations.json are the canonical source-of-truth. js/saints-resolver.js provides the shared resolver boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. Phase 4 duplicate identity consolidation complete (v2.8.8): 46 pairs resolved, 1,228 identities remain. COE audit and governance complete (v2.8.9). Remaining: normalized-source runtime adapter (SaintsResolver reads monthly cache files at runtime; a true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly).

5. [CLOSED — v2.8.9] COE saint/commemoration rendering: Three-layer rebuild complete. Layer 1 (season engine) was already operational. Layer 2 (fixed feasts: Sawma Rabba Friday martyrs, pre-Sawma Commemoration of the Faithful Departed) implemented in COE-IIA via getDayClass() in calendar-east-syriac.js. Layer 3 (sparse individual saints) source-data corrected via COE-IIB audit (133 IDs removed), eligibility gate implemented in js/coe-eligibility.js (CoeEligibility.filter()), and display re-enabled in renderEastSyriac(). saint-grid model eliminated. CoeEligibility.filter() is mandatory before any Layer 3 render. Layer 2 output from getDayClass() renders before Layer 3 lookup. No saint-grid fallback. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present.

6. EO old/new calendar mode is partially implemented in admin-only tooling (CalendarEngine.getEOSeasonRanges() + admin/admin.html snapshots), but the main application still has no EO mode UI and does not load js/calendar-eastern-orthodox.js on the production path.

7. Duplicate private helper definition in js/horologion-engine.js: _loadTheotokionData() appears twice. Runtime is currently safe because the later definition wins, but the duplicate is architectural debris and should be removed to prevent misleading future edits.
resolved_in_this_version: 1. COE saint section silenced: renderEastSyriac() saint display locked to empty pending architectural rebuild. Previous behaviour was historically inaccurate — string-matching tradition fields was surfacing wrong saints. Silence is liturgically correct for COE weekdays with no fixed saint.

key_logic_notes: 1. Reading resolution uses independent morning/evening chains — no shared intermediate variables between MP and EP
2. Theotokion uses two-step lookup: cop-theotokion-${season} with fallback to cop-theotokion

3. Examen text: \n\n converted to

, rendered in

4. toggleSidebar() detects active panel by checking which is NOT mode-hidden — priority order: East Syriac → Ethiopian → Generic → BCP (fallback). Now supports four sidebar identities including #generic-settings.
5. CalendarEngine.init() called explicitly in init() after JSON loads, before fetchLectionaryData()
6. resolvedOfficeId = window._forcedOfficeId || officeId — used throughout renderOffice() to support ethiopian-saatat override

7. SaintsResolver monthly cache warmed via resolveCommemorations() before sequence loop. OOR fallback filters use SaintsResolver.filterCachedByTradition(currentDate, 'OOR') — synchronous, no fetch. appData.saints is no longer used.
8. isEthiopianSaatat flag gates all BCP-specific pre/post-sequence blocks
9. getEthiopianHourInfo() returns exhaustive 9-entry 24-hour map covering all canonical watches
10. window._temporalOverride — date and hourId override for testing any canonical hour at any date
11. MONTH_SLUG_MAP in eth-saints-commemoration normalises EthiopianCalendar month names to folder slugs; handles miyazya→miazia and teqemt→tiqimt irregularities
12. Senkessar index and per-day narratives cached in appData.senkessarIndex and appData.senkessarCache — no redundant fetches
13. renderEastSyriac() uses EastSyriacCalendar.getSeason(currentDate) for season, cycle, cycleLabel, and seasonColor
14. East Syriac routing: selectedTime drives sequence selection; officeMode (cathedral/monastic) drives variant; else-default is always Ramsha

15. selectMode('horologion') hides #settings-panel, #ethiopian-settings, and #east-syriac-settings, then removes mode-hidden/sidebar-hidden from #generic-settings and removes sidebar-hidden from #main-content. Calls updateGenericDateDisplay() and _updateHorologionOfficeButtons(). selectedHorologionOffice defaults to 'vespers' on first entry.
16. backToSplash() adds mode-hidden + sidebar-hidden to all four sidebar panels (including #generic-settings). No panel is left active on splash return.

17. updateGenericDateDisplay() — syncs #generic-display-date (long locale string), #generic-date-picker (YYYY-MM-DD), and #generic-calendar-info. Called by selectMode('horologion'), changeDate(), resetDate(), and setCustomDate() when selectedMode === 'horologion'.
18. selectHorologionOffice(officeKey) — sets selectedHorologionOffice, calls _updateHorologionOfficeButtons(), calls requestRender().

19. _updateHorologionOfficeButtons() — highlights the active office button in #generic-settings for offices ['vespers','small-compline','first-hour','third-hour','sixth-hour','ninth-hour','orthros']. Updated in v5.6 to include 'orthros'.

20. _horologionOfficeLabel(officeKey) — returns display label for officeKey: 'vespers'→'Vespers', 'small-compline'→'Small Compline', 'first-hour'→'First Hour', 'third-hour'→'Third Hour', 'sixth-hour'→'Sixth Hour', 'ninth-hour'→'Ninth Hour', 'orthros'→'Orthros'. Updated in v5.6.

21. renderHorologionOffice() routes 'orthros' to HorologionEngine.resolveOffice() via _resolveOrthrosSlots(); season-aware god-is-the-lord / Alleluia path determined from _computeLiturgicalSeason() output (v5.6–v5.8).

js/calendar-engine.js.role: All date and liturgical calendar logic
js/calendar-engine.js.status: Current — v2.8.9 + 2026-03-06 admin EO diagnostics patch
js/calendar-engine.js.architecture_note: v2.8.1: Hardcoded SEASON_RANGES array (was valid through Dec 2030 only) replaced with algorithmic generator. getLiturgicalYear() is now fully algorithmic. Both are valid for any year indefinitely. Admin/devtool extension now adds EO snapshot helpers for old_calendar vs new_calendar diagnostics; this does not by itself expose EO mode in the main app.
public_methods: init() — loads bcp-propers.json; must be called before fetchLectionaryData()
getSeasonAndFile(date) — returns { season, file, liturgicalColor, litYear }
getLiturgicalYear(date) — returns 'year1' or 'year2'; algorithmic, anchored to Advent 2025 = Year 2
getCurrentProper(date) — returns Proper number 3–29
fetchLectionaryData(date) — returns daily entry object from seasonal JSON
getCurrentDate()
setCurrentDate(date)
changeDate(days)
resetDate()
formatDateForLookup(date) — returns 'February 17, 2026'
formatDateISO(date) — returns '2026-02-17'
getYearSnapshot(year, config) — admin/devtool snapshot adapter; routes EOR to EasternOrthodoxCalendar when available
getEOSeasonRanges(year, eoMode) — admin/devtool EO fixed-feast + season-range diagnostics for new_calendar vs old_calendar
js/calendar-engine.js.backward_compatibility: SEASON_RANGES static getter retained — returns algorithmically generated ranges for current and prior calendar year. Any external code referencing CalendarEngine.SEASON_RANGES continues to work.

js/calendar-east-syriac.js.role: Church of the East liturgical calendar — nine seasons, Qdham/Wathar cycle, seasonal colour
js/calendar-east-syriac.js.status: COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL — All 20 self-tests passing (confirmed in console 2026-02-27)
public_methods: getSeason(date) — returns { season, weekInSeason, cycle, weekLabel, seasonLabel, easter }
getDayClass(date) — returns full day-classification object including isFriday, isLenten, isNinevehFast, dayClass, commemorations[], and all getSeason fields
getEaster(gregorianYear) — returns Date (Gregorian JS Date of d'Qyamta for that year)
getLiturgicalYear(date) — returns { subaraStart, easter, seasons, nextSubara }
getFixedCommemorationsForDate(date) — returns commemoration records for Layer 2 fixed observances
SEASON_META — read-only season metadata object
seasons: eliya-sliwa — Elijah and the Holy Cross
js/calendar-east-syriac.js.cycle_note: Qdham (Before) and Wathar (After) are the two-week rotating cycles anchored to Subara Sunday. Drives Qanona, Marmitha, and Barekmar selection.

js/calendar-ethiopian.js.role: Ethiopian (Ge'ez) calendar conversion and date resolution
js/calendar-ethiopian.js.status: COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL — Phase 7.4. Fully integrated.
public_methods: getEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns { day, month, monthIndex, year } in Amete Mihret era
getCopticDate(gregorianDate) — same algorithm, Coptic month names, EC-276 era
formatEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns display string e.g. '15 Yekatit 2018'
isEthiopianLeapYear(ethiopianYear) — boolean; leap when year % 4 === 3
MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Ge'ez month name strings
COPTIC_MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Coptic month name strings
implementation_notes: 1. Julian Day Number (JDN) method — valid across all historical and future dates
2. Epoch: 1 Meskerem 1 EC = JDN 1724221 = 29 August 8 AD (Julian)
3. Pagume: 5 days normally, 6 in leap years (year % 4 === 3)
4. Self-test suite on load — 9 verified reference points logged to console

js/horologion-engine.js.architecture_note: Horologion v5.8 (current). v3.5: feast-rank arbitration introduced — _resolveFeastOverrideContext() governs Vespers troparion/apolytikion and dismissal Theotokion. v3.6: _resolveLittleHourTroparionSlot() extends feast-rank override to Little Hours troparion-of-the-day. v3.7: _resolveFestalRubricOverrideSlot() applies qualifying feast rubric override to Vespers stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried and aposticha. v3.8: Great Lent seasonal layer added inside _resolveLittleHourSeasonalTroparionSlot() — displaces ordinary weekday fallback on Great Lent Mon–Fri for all four Little Hours. v3.9: _resolveComplineSeasonalTroparionSlot() applies parallel Great Lent seasonal override for Small Compline troparion-of-the-day. v4.0: _normalizeOrdinaryTroparionFallbackForOffice() rewrites the inherited Vespers weekday-theme-rubric into office-appropriate text for Small Compline and all Little Hours. v4.1/v4.3: _normalizeTheotokionRubricForOffice() + _resolveLittleHourFestalTheotokionRubric() — Little Hours Theotokion normalization and festal rubric arbitration for First through Ninth Hour. v4.4: _resolveComplineFestalTheotokionRubric() — Small Compline festal Theotokion rubric arbitration. _normalizeUnavailableTroparionFallbackForOffice() rewrites menaion-text-unavailable fallback rubrics to use the consuming office name rather than Vespers wording; invoked as the first call inside _normalizeOrdinaryTroparionFallbackForOffice() and independently at the base of _resolveLittleHourSeasonalTroparionSlot() and _resolveComplineSeasonalTroparionSlot(). v5.0: _loadTriodionData() + _resolveTriodionTroparion() — Triodion Great Lent weekday overlay; fires after feast arbitration on Great Lent weekdays when no qualifying feast resolved; corpus covers Weeks 1–6 (data/triodion/triodion-lenten-weekday.json). v5.1: _loadHolyWeekData() + _resolveHolyWeekText() — Holy Week text overlay; troparion slot populated all 7 Holy Week days (data/triodion/holy-week/holy-week.json); stichera/aposticha/paremiae/theotokion remain as informed rubrics. v5.2: _loadPentecostarionData() + _resolvePentecostarionText() — Pentecostarion Bright Week stichera and aposticha (data/pentecostarion/pentecostarion-bright-week.json); wired into stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried and aposticha Bright Week branches in _resolveVespersSlots(). v5.4: _renderHorologionDiagnostics(item, escapeHtml) added to office-ui.js — optional inline diagnostics annotation for variable Horologion slots. Wired into rubric, stichera, and fallback-text branches of _renderHorologionItem(). Controlled by _horDiagnosticsEnabled flag; toggle via sidebar button or console. v5.5: _loadKathismaFullTextData() + upgraded _resolveVespersKathismaSlot() — ordinary weekday Vespers kathisma slots now resolve to full Psalter text where corpus exists (kathismata 1,4,6,8,10,12 supported in kathisma-full-text.json); Great Lent guard returns great-lent-ordinary-cycle-inapplicable; Bright Week and Sunday Small Vespers return explicit omission rubrics. v5.6: _resolveOrthrosSlots() added — Orthros structural baseline. Fixed content: Usual Beginning, Six Psalms, Praises Psalms, Great Doxology resolved from orthros-fixed.json. god-is-the-lord / Alleluia path resolved via _computeLiturgicalSeason() across ordinary, Great Lent weekday, Holy Week, Bright Week. Troparion delegates to existing feast/seasonal stack. SUPPORTED_OFFICES expanded to include 'orthros'. v5.7: _resolveOrthrosKathismaSlots() + _resolveOrthrosPraisesSticheraSlot() — kathisma appointments and Praises stichera upgraded from placeholders to explicit day-of-week / season-aware rubrics using orthros-kathisma.json. Orthros: 18 implemented / 2 placeholders. v5.8: _resolveOrthrosSessionalHymnsSlot() + _resolveOrthrosCanonSlot() — sessional hymns and canon upgraded from vague placeholders to season-aware structural rubric logic distinguishing ordinary weekday, Sunday, Great Lent weekday, Holy Week, and Bright Week. Feast inheritance intact. Orthros: 20 implemented / 0 placeholder slots.

js/menaion-resolver.js.role: Menaion pilot resolver — query boundary between the Horologion engine and Menaion date corpora
public_methods: queryTroparion(mmdd) — Promise<ResultObject>. Accepts 'MM-DD' string. Returns status + troparion text (if resolved) or fallback code. Non-throwing.
isMonthImported(monthNum) — boolean. Returns true only if the month's JSON has been registered in MONTH_FILES.
importedMonths() — returns array of imported month numbers.
result_status_codes: menaion-resolved — text found; full troparion text + name + tone + rank returned
menaion-text-unavailable — commemoration identified but troparion text not yet in corpus
menaion-not-imported — month has not been imported into the pilot corpus
menaion-no-ranked-commemoration — date imported but no commemoration meets minimum rank threshold
menaion-load-error — JSON fetch failed; engine falls through to theme rubric
js/menaion-resolver.js.architecture_note: IIFE pattern (same as HorologionEngine). Soft dependency: if this file fails to load or the query throws, horologion-engine.js degrades silently to the weekday-theme rubric. This module owns all Menaion data loading; the engine owns none. Rank selection rule: if multiple commemorations exist on a date, the highest-rank entry (lowest integer) is selected; ties resolved by first entry. CURRENT SCOPE: COMPLETE — all 12 months imported. November (30/30), December (31/31), January (31/31), February (28/28), March (31/31), April (30/30), May (31/31), June (30/30), July (31/31), August (31/31), September (30/30), October (31/31). No month returns 'menaion-not-imported'. Full annual fixed-date Menaion corpus is present. Feast-rank override engine live through engine v3.5–v4.4 (see horologion-engine.js). Triodion, Holy Week, and Pentecostarion seasonal overlays live through v5.0–v5.2. Corpus audit complete through v5.3: 369/370 resolved; 05-18 intentional holdout.

saints_model.status_note: All 12 monthly cache files are present as of v2.8.2 and last regenerated in v2.8.8 (Phase 4 duplicate consolidation). js/saints-resolver.js (SaintsResolver) is the sole monthly-cache owner and the canonical saints boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. No appData.saints usage or direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js. Phase 4 duplicate identity consolidation complete: 46 pairs resolved, 1,228 unique identities remain.
saints_model.workflow: Edit identities.json or commemorations.json → run `npm run saints:regen` (validates then regenerates all months) → commit modified source files and all regenerated monthly cache files together. To regenerate a single month: `node tools/build_saints_cache.js <month>`.
rules: Do not edit saints-<month>.json files manually.
All dates in source-of-truth files must use numeric { month, day }.
Tradition is defined only in commemorations.json.
ECU is derived by generator when identity has commemorations in all five traditions on the same date.
Generated cache records carry a `type` field derived from identity.type. Do not flatten type distinctions (saint / feast / commemoration / apostle / prophet / marian_feast) at the UI layer.
unfinished_work: Normalized-source runtime adapter: SaintsResolver reads generated cache files at runtime. A true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly.

hydration_race_condition.description: RESOLVED — v2.8.6 (doc closure). The hydration-race gating layer is implemented and verified in code. renderOffice() returns early unless isHydrationComplete is true. selectMode() awaits the correct hydration function before setting isHydrationComplete = true and calling requestRender(). requestRender() / flushRender() coalesce concurrent render requests and serialise execution via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration.
hydration_race_condition.residual_note: No automated regression test covers the isHydrationComplete gating path. A future test pass should add coverage to guard against silent regressions if the boot sequence is refactored.

decisions.title: Byzantine Tradition Profile Governance
decisions.summary: The Universal Office Horologion is governed as multi-tradition capable. The first fully implemented recension remains Slavic Byzantine, but the architecture is designed to support additional Byzantine tradition profiles (e.g. Antiochian, Greek) through profile-specific corpora and rubrical overlays.
details: Office structure and resolver architecture remain common across traditions wherever possible.
Textual corpora such as Octoechos, Menaion, and other hymnographic collections may be tradition-profile specific.
Future tradition profiles should be implemented through data overlays rather than separate engines.

decisions.title: Octoechos Corpus Namespace Standard
decisions.summary: Octoechos hymnographic corpora are normalized under a single namespace keyed by office → family → case → tone.
details: Octoechos data should live under window.OCTOECHOS.
Repetitive hymnographic items may be stored as compact string arrays and expanded into structured objects by the resolver at runtime.

decisions.title: Octoechos Tone-of-Week Computation
decisions.summary: The Octoechos tone of the week is computed algorithmically from Pascha rather than stored by date in corpus files.
details: The weekly tone cycle begins with Tone 1 on Thomas Sunday and advances by one each week, cycling 1–8.
Bright Week suspends the ordinary Octoechos weekly cycle.
Corpus files store hymnography by tone, not by civil date.
Seasonal and festal layers may override tone-governed hymnography, but they do not replace the anchor calculation.

decisions.title: Liturgical Education Layer
decisions.summary: The Universal Office is governed as both a devotional tool and a pedagogical tool. Educational explanation is a first-class architectural layer, not incidental UI ornament.
details: Each prayer tradition should support educational explanation appropriate to its own structure and terminology.
Educational content should distinguish terminology, office structure, and tradition-specific practice.
The system should support layered explanation: micro-explanation, structural explanation, and tradition explanation.
Educational notes must clarify differences between traditions without flattening them.
